course_title: Design Computing Studio I - Interactive Technology

learning_outcomes.description: Analyse and propose design computing solutions that aligns with the objectives and constraints of a project brief.
Understand key course concepts and studio workflow and apply those principles in an iterative development process.
Apply design and technical skills within a multidisciplinary team to build a proof-of-concept prototype.
Apply critical thinking to reflect on and analyse the development and outcome of a project, considering how well they align with user needs and the project brief.
Recognise ethical implications of design decisions and describe their potential impact on users, communities, and stakeholders.
Apply teamwork and communication strategies to collaborate effectively, resolve conflict, and contribute to shared project goals.
Present design decisions, design process and outcomes clearly through visual, oral and written communication.

assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Participation/ Student contribution, Reflection, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessments.task_description:
Each week, you will be assigned design activities linked to weekly studio and practical sessions that support the development of design knowledge, technical skills and project work. Activities may include design exercises, critiques, readings, reflections, technical challenges, or responses to weekly course content. The activities provide the opportunity to apply concepts introduced in class, document learning as it occurs, and receive feedback on developing ideas and skills.

Together, the design activities provide evidence of your individual engagement, progress and contributions throughout the semester. They also support the development of your team project and Design Portfolio.

Use of AI and Machine Translation tools

This assessment is designed to help students develop and apply user-centred design methods, design thinking, and reflective practice in authentic studio contexts. Success in this assessment requires active participation in design activities, critical engagement with users, teams, and project challenges, and the ability to apply course concepts to specific design situations.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use Generative AI technologies, successful completion of this task will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

Generative AI Tools and techniques may be utilized as supportive elements. However, there are boundaries to your usage of Generative AI Tools. Going beyond those boundaries amounts to an academic integrity issue. 

Where applicable, students will be required to attach a Statement of Work to their design activity outcome that explicitly identifies both original and non-original work in the submission; and clearly describes any use of AI or MT used to inform the design or the production of the work.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Activity outcomes are to be uploaded to your Design Portfolio padlet unless specified otherwise for a specific activity.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.mode: Activity/ Performance, Oral, Product/ Artefact/ Multimedia, Written
assessments.category: Computer Code, Creative Production/ Exhibition, Essay/ Critique, Practical/ Demonstration, Presentation, Product/ Design, Project, Reflection
assessments.task_description:
Students will work in small teams to design and prototype a design computing solution to a given brief over the semester.

To support team and project progress, there will be 3 key milestones for the project:

  1. Proposal Presentation
  2. Presentation of proposed project direction in studio class in Week 5
  3. Submission of slides + response to feedback Friday Week 5
  4. Work-in-Progress Demonstration
  5. Demonstration of work-in-progress in studio class in Week 9
  6. Submission of link to work, response to feedback and plan for completion Friday Week 9
  7. Final Tradeshow
  8. Tradeshow demonstration of proof-of-concept prototype in studio class Week 12
  9. Project Debrief Report due Week 13

Grading and feedback on milestones 1 & 2 will be formative, intended to provide students with an understanding of the quality of their work against the criteria, and to inform and support their subsequent work to refine and improve their project based on the feedback. The overall grade for the project will be considered holistically, examining work across the whole lifetime of the project, but with an emphasis in particular on the endpoint and on the quality of the process followed to get there.

Second assessment sequence approved by the Associate Dean (Academic)

Use of AI and Machine Translation tools in this assessment

This assessment is designed to help students apply user-centred design processes to a complex, open-ended design challenge. Success in this assessment requires students to engage with users, generate and evaluate design ideas, make informed design decisions, develop and refine prototypes, and collaborate effectively within a project team.

The project has been designed to be authentic, collaborative, and iterative. While Generative AI tools may be used to support activities such as brainstorming, research, ideation, coding assistance, content generation, or communication, successful completion of the project requires design judgement, critical evaluation, user-centred decision-making, and teamwork that cannot be delegated to artificial intelligence.

Generative AI tools may be used as supportive elements throughout the design process. However, students remain responsible for all project outcomes and must be able to explain, justify, and critically evaluate the design decisions, artefacts, and technical implementations presented in their work.

The final design solution, underlying design rationale, and evidence of user-centred design practice must reflect the student's and team's own understanding, engagement, and contribution. Use of Generative AI beyond these boundaries may constitute an academic integrity matter.

All use of Generative AI must comply with course guidance and be appropriately acknowledged. Teams will be required to include a Statement of Work in their application that explicitly identifies both original and non-original work in the submission; and clearly describes any use of AI or MT used to inform the design or the production of the work.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Project documents will be submitted via Blackboard. Presentations and demonstrations will take place during the scheduled studio session.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
The aim of this submission is to support the development of your final Reflective Design Portfolio and provide an opportunity for feedback before the end of semester.

You will submit a draft of your portfolio that documents your learning, project contributions, and development to date. The submission should include selected evidence from studio activities and project work, reflections on your progress and learning, and a plan outlining what you intend to achieve, document, and reflect upon during the remainder of the semester.

Because this assessment evaluates your developing reflective and design practice, you must clearly acknowledge any external tools, including Generative AI, used in preparing portfolio content.

The feedback provided is formative and intended to help you strengthen the quality of your evidence, reflections, and portfolio structure before the final submission.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Please submit the URL for your portfolio at PadletUQ to the submission link on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
9/11/2026 5:00 pm

Final deadline for updates to the Design Portfolio is 5:00pm Monday Exam Week 1.

The Portfolio Review Interview forms the secure component of the Design Portfolio assessment. Please see the Portfolio Review Interview item for information about what is required and when these will be scheduled.
assessments.task_description:
The Design Portfolio documents your individual learning, contributions, and development throughout the semester. Drawing on evidence collected during studio activities and project work, the portfolio should showcase key examples of your design practice, including your process, decision-making, skills development, and contribution to the team project.

More than a collection of artefacts, the portfolio is an annotated reflection on your design journey. You should curate and present selected evidence of your work, explaining what you did, why you did it, what you learned, and how your thinking evolved through collaboration, feedback, and iteration.

The portfolio is both a record of your work and a design artefact in its own right. It should be organised, visually effective, and communicate your individual contribution within the broader context of the team project. Strong portfolios demonstrate not only what was produced, but also how challenges were addressed, decisions were made, and learning occurred throughout the semester.

The portfolio will be developed through the following milestones:

  1. Portfolio Draft & Plan due in Week 7, at this checkpoint, your will submit a draft of your portfolio, including a progress review, reflection on learning to date and a plan for the remainder of the semester. See assessment item Portfolio Draft & Plan for more information.
  2. Design Portfolio due in Exam Week 1, you will submit your final portfolio demonstrating contributions, learning, professional growth and critical reflection across the semester. You are encouraged to draw on evidence generated through the Design Activities assessment, project activities, workshop exercises, feedback, and project artefacts to support your reflections and demonstrate learning throughout the semester. See assessment item Design Portfolio for more information.
  3. Portfolio Review Interview in Exam Week 1, following the submission of their final portfolio, you will undertake an interview with teaching staff about your learning and contributions across the semester. Your portfolio will provide the evidence of your work. See assessment item Portfolio Review Interview for more information.

Use of MT/AI Tools for producing the Portfolio

This assessment is designed to help students critically reflect on their learning, design practice, and contributions to the team project throughout the semester. Success in this assessment requires students to interpret and analyse their own experiences, evaluate their growth as designers, and support their reflections with evidence drawn from their work.

The portfolio is a personal and reflective assessment. While Generative AI tools may be used to support activities such as brainstorming, structuring content, editing, or improving clarity of expression, they cannot substitute for the student's own reflection, analysis, judgement, or lived experience within the course.This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use Generative AI technologies, successful completion of this task will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

Generative AI Tools and techniques may be utilized as supportive elements. However, there are boundaries to your usage of Generative AI Tools. Going beyond those boundaries amounts to an academic integrity issue. 

Students will be required to include a Statement of Work in their portfolio that explicitly identifies both original and non-original work in the submission; and clearly describes any use of AI or MT used to inform the design or the production of the work.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You will produce and maintain your portfolio using PadletUQ and will submit the link to Blackboard. Instructions for how to set up your Padlet portfolio is available on Blackboard. It is your responsibility to ensure that Padlet links are accessible by teaching staff.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
10/11/2026 - 12/11/2026

Portfolio Review Interview sessions will take place Tuesday to Thursday in Exam Week 1. Sign-up for sessions will be available from Week 12 to assist your planning around exams. As this is a secure, identity-verified assessment, you will need to produce valid UQ identification before commencing your interview.
assessments.task_description:
Following the submission of your final portfolio, you will undertake a Portfolio Review Interview. During this interview, you will have 3-5 minutes to present the highlights from your portfolio as a precursor to a 5 -7 minute Q&A session with teaching staff. Questions will focus on understanding the contributions you made to the team project and your learning in the course. You should focus reflections on the work you have completed through course activities, through self-directed learning and through the project - reflecting on your role in the success (or failure) of the team project. You will use the content of your portfolio to evidence the work you have done.

Use of MT/AI Tools for the Portfolio presentation & interview

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

In accordance with UQ Assessment Policy, your presentation will be recorded.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Studio

Studios will introduce theory related to design theory and practice to be applied through in-class activities to project work. Students will work with teaching staff on their team projects and on activities related to their projects.

The final Tradeshow will take place in the studio session in Week 12, where teams will demonstrate their projects to a broader audience.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07

Students will work with teaching staff on their team projects and on activities related to their projects. The final Tradeshow will take place in the studio session in Week 12, where teams will demonstrate their projects to a broader audience.

Practical

Practical sessions will support individual work towards the team project through technical and design exercises. Students will also have the opportunity to receive feedback & guidance from their demonstrator on their work.
